title: Choosing Your Vocab Size
concept: Match vocabulary size to your dataset and hardware: smaller vocab for monolingual/lightweight tasks; larger for multilingual/generative. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Out-of-Vocabulary Handling.'

exercises.prompt: What does "vocabulary size" refer to in a text AI model?

options.text: How many unique words or subwords the model can recognize
The total number of books the model has read
The speed at which the model types answers

exercises.explain: Vocabulary size dictates how many unique words or word-parts a model's brain can hold in memory.

cards.title: Choosing Your Vocab Size
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI decides how many words it should learn? Teach it too few, and it gets confused. Teach it too many, and its brain runs out of memory! Let's find the sweet spot.

cards.title: What is Vocabulary Size?
cards.body: Vocabulary size is the total number of unique words or word-pieces that an AI model is allowed to know. Choosing the right size determines how fast the AI runs and how much memory it uses.

cards.title: The Toolbox Analogy
cards.body: Think of your vocabulary size like a toolbox. If you are only fixing a bicycle (a small, single-language task), a small toolbox with 10 tools is light and perfect. But if you are building a spaceship (a massive multilingual system), you need a massive garage with thousands of specialized tools.

cards.title: Small vs. Large Vocabularies
cards.body: If you are building a simple chat app that only speaks English, a vocab size of 30,000 subwords works great. But a global translator like Google Translate might use 100,000+ subwords to cover Chinese, Arabic, French, and English together!

cards.title: Is Bigger Always Better?
cards.body: People often think a bigger vocabulary always makes an AI smarter. In reality, a massive vocabulary makes the model run much slower and take up way too much phone or computer memory. Bigger isn't always better!

cards.title: The Balancing Act
cards.body: Compare how vocabulary choices affect performance and memory depending on your project scale.

right.text: Slower speeds, high memory usage, needed for multi-language systems.

left.text: Faster speeds, low memory usage, perfect for single languages.

exercises.prompt: Why would you choose a smaller vocabulary size for your AI model?

options.text: To save computer memory and run faster on small devices.
To make the AI write in ten different languages.
To guarantee the model never makes spelling mistakes.
To increase the size of the computer's hard drive.

exercises.explain: Smaller vocabularies require less memory, making them ideal for lightweight or resource-constrained devices like mobile phones.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A model trained on five languages generally needs a larger vocabulary than a model trained on only English.
exercises.explain: Multilingual models need to recognize characters and words from many different languages, so they require larger vocabularies.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the design guideline.
exercises.sentence: You should use a larger vocabulary size for a multilingual model than a monolingual one.
exercises.explain: Multilingual models need to understand many different alphabets and words, requiring a much larger vocabulary size.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about setting vocabulary sizes is correct?

options.text: AI models automatically shrink their vocabulary size to 100 words when your phone battery is low.
A small vocabulary works well for a lightweight, single-language task.
Every AI model in the world uses the exact same vocabulary size of 50,000.

exercises.explain: The first option is AI-generated nonsense. Tokenizer vocabulary sizes do not change dynamically based on the battery percentage of a smartphone.

exercises.prompt: When designing a language model, what is the best strategy for choosing your vocabulary size?

options.text: Always choose the largest possible vocabulary size.
Balance task complexity and hardware limits.
Vocab size does not affect computer memory.
Smaller vocab sizes are only used for translation.

exercises.explain: A massive vocabulary size sounds great, but it requires much more computer memory and slower processing speeds. We must balance our dataset size, languages, and hardware! Next up: Out-of-Vocabulary Handling.
